title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
text: evaluate whether variance-weighting improves clustering quality, need independent ground truth — one derived RNA clusters . use cbmc CITE-seq dataset SeuratData, measured RNA surface protein (ADT) 8,617 cells. ADT-based cell type labels (protein_annotations) assigned gating canonical surface markers (CD4, CD8, CD14, CD16, CD19, CD56, CD34) — measurement entirely independent RNA clustering evaluating. Two metrics computed method:

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
code: library(Seurat) library(SeuratData) library(ggplot2) library(patchwork) library(wUMAP) library(mclust)   # adjustedRandIndex library(RANN)     # fast k-NN data(cbmc) cbmc <- UpdateSeuratObject(cbmc)  # Keep only cells with protein labels; remove doublets cbmc <- cbmc[, !cbmc$protein_annotations %in% "T/Mono doublets" &                !is.na(cbmc$protein_annotations)]  cbmc <- NormalizeData(cbmc, verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindVariableFeatures(cbmc, nfeatures = 2000, verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- ScaleData(cbmc, verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunPCA(cbmc, npcs = 30, verbose = FALSE) knn_purity <- function(emb, labels, k = 20) {   nn  <- RANN::nn2(emb, k = k + 1)$nn.idx[, -1]   lab <- as.integer(factor(labels))   mean(sapply(seq_len(nrow(nn)), function(i) mean(lab[nn[i, ]] == lab[i]))) }

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
code: set.seed(42) cbmc <- FindNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, resolution = 0.8, verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, reduction.name = "umap.std", verbose = FALSE)  ari_std <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_std <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.std"), cbmc$protein_annotations)

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
code: set.seed(42)  # stdev weighting cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev",                              prefix = "wt.sd", verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.sd_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev",                         reduction.name = "umap.sd", verbose = FALSE) ari_sd <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_sd <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.sd"), cbmc$protein_annotations)  # stdev + log.scale cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev",                              log.scale = TRUE, prefix = "wt.sd.log",                              verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.sd.log_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev",                         log.scale = TRUE, reduction.name = "umap.sd.log",                         verbose = FALSE) ari_sd_log <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_sd_log <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.sd.log"), cbmc$protein_annotations)  # prop.var, weight.factor = 0.5 cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                              weight.factor = 0.5, prefix = "wt.pv05",                              verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.pv05_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         weight.factor = 0.5, reduction.name = "umap.pv05",                         verbose = FALSE) ari_pv05 <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_pv05 <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.pv05"), cbmc$protein_annotations)  # prop.var, weight.factor = 1 cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                              prefix = "wt.pv", verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.pv_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         reduction.name = "umap.pv", verbose = FALSE) ari_pv <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_pv <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.pv"), cbmc$protein_annotations)  # prop.var + log.scale cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                              log.scale = TRUE, prefix = "wt.pv.log",                              verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.pv.log_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         log.scale = TRUE, reduction.name = "umap.pv.log",                         verbose = FALSE) ari_pv_log <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_pv_log <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.pv.log"), cbmc$protein_annotations)  # Marchenko-Pastur weighting cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "mp",                              prefix = "wt.mp", verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.mp_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "mp",                         reduction.name = "umap.mp", verbose = FALSE) ari_mp <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_mp <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.mp"), cbmc$protein_annotations)

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
text: RunLocalPCAUMAP() embedding-method — builds per-neighbourhood PCA basis uses compute anisotropic local distances running UMAP. companion graph-building step, k-NN purity (direct measure UMAP embedding quality) reported; ARI left NA.
code: set.seed(42) cbmc <- RunLocalPCAUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 30,                         reduction.name = "umap.lp", verbose = FALSE) pur_lp <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.lp"), cbmc$protein_annotations)

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
text: Clustering quality vs protein labels cbmc CITE-seq (n ≈ 7,699 cells). ARI = NA local PCA UMAP (embedding-method). ARI k-NN purity method. Dashed line = standard UMAP baseline. ARI shown local PCA UMAP (embedding-method).
code: results <- data.frame(   Method = c(     "standard (none)",     "stdev",     "stdev + log.scale",     "prop.var  wf=0.5",     "prop.var  wf=1",     "prop.var + log.scale",     "mp (Marchenko-Pastur)",     "local PCA UMAP"   ),   ARI       = c(ari_std, ari_sd, ari_sd_log, ari_pv05, ari_pv, ari_pv_log,                 ari_mp, NA_real_),   kNN_purity = c(pur_std, pur_sd, pur_sd_log, pur_pv05, pur_pv, pur_pv_log,                  pur_mp, pur_lp) ) knitr::kable(results, digits = 3,   caption = "Clustering quality vs protein labels on cbmc CITE-seq (n ≈ 7,699 cells). ARI = NA for local PCA UMAP (embedding-only method).") library(tidyr)  res_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer(results, cols = c("ARI", "kNN_purity"),                                 names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Score") res_long$Method <- factor(res_long$Method, levels = results$Method)  baseline <- data.frame(   Metric = c("ARI", "kNN_purity"),   Score  = c(ari_std, pur_std) )  ggplot(res_long[!is.na(res_long$Score), ],        aes(x = Method, y = Score, fill = Method)) +   geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +   geom_hline(data = baseline, aes(yintercept = Score),              linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   facet_wrap(~Metric, scales = "free_y") +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   coord_flip() +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,        title = "wUMAP benchmark on cbmc CITE-seq",        subtitle = "Dashed line = standard UMAP baseline") +   theme_bw(base_size = 12)

what: UMAP layouts coloured by protein label
title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
text: methods coloured ADT-derived protein label.
code: Idents(cbmc) <- "protein_annotations"  plots <- list(   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.std",   label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("standard  ARI=%.3f", ari_std))    + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.sd",    label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("stdev  ARI=%.3f", ari_sd))        + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.sd.log", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("stdev+log  ARI=%.3f", ari_sd_log))+ NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.pv05",  label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("prop.var wf=0.5  ARI=%.3f", ari_pv05)) + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.pv",    label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("prop.var wf=1  ARI=%.3f", ari_pv)) + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.pv.log", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("prop.var+log  ARI=%.3f", ari_pv_log)) + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.mp",    label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("mp  ARI=%.3f", ari_mp)) + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.lp",    label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("local PCA UMAP  purity=%.3f", pur_lp)) + NoLegend() )  wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 2)

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
text: PBMC-like dataset, aggressive prop.var weighting (wf=1) reduces clustering quality (ARI 0.548 → 0.318). PC 1 scRNA-seq captures monocyte–lymphocyte axis strongly emphasising collapses finer lymphocyte subtypes together. Key takeaways: stdev safest default — marginal ARI change, k-NN purity standard. prop.var weight.factor = 0.5 (partial blend) avoids worst -emphasising PC 1. log.scale = TRUE partially mitigates PC 1 dominance recovers ARI compared full prop.var. mp (Marchenko–Pastur) automatically zeros noise PCs retains variance random-matrix noise ceiling. datasets PCs genuine signal behaves like prop.var; noisier datasets can sharpen clusters discarding uninformative PCs entirely. Local PCA UMAP evaluated ARI (build clustering graph), k-NN purity reflects well embedding locally preserves cell-type neighbourhoods. can expose trajectory-like geometry global metrics miss. k-NN purity differences generally small across methods, indicating UMAP topology broadly preserved — main effect Louvain cluster resolution. Recommendation: start weight.= "stdev" weight.= "prop.var", weight.factor = 0.5, inspect cluster marker genes judge whether weighting helping obscuring biology dataset. Use weight.= "mp" want principled, data-driven noise threshold. Use RunLocalPCAUMAP() suspect dataset strong local anisotropic structure (trajectories, gradients) global metric miss.

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: Standard UMAP treats every principal component (PC) equally. scRNA-seq, early PCs capture far biological variation later ones — yet standard UMAP PCs 1–50 weights PC 50 PC 1. wUMAP scales PC axis weight derived variance contribution handing embedding UMAP, biologically informative PCs dominate cell distances final layout.

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
code: library(Seurat) library(SeuratData) library(ggplot2) library(wUMAP) library(patchwork)

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: use classic PBMC 3k dataset SeuratData, includes PCA, cell-type annotations, standard clustering already applied.
code: data(pbmc3k.final) pbmc <- UpdateSeuratObject(pbmc3k.final) Idents(pbmc) <- "seurat_annotations"

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: RunWeightedUMAP() drop-replacement RunUMAP(). Setting weight.= "none" reproduces standard result; weight.= "prop.var" (default) scales PC proportion explained variance. Left: standard UMAP. Right: variance-weighted UMAP.
code: set.seed(42) pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "none",                         reduction.name = "umap.std",     verbose = FALSE)  pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "prop.var",                         reduction.name = "umap.wt",      verbose = FALSE) p1 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.std", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Standard UMAP") + NoLegend() p2 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wt",  label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Weighted UMAP (prop.var)") + NoLegend() p1 | p2

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: Three schemes available via weight.: "mp" option applies Marchenko–Pastur (MP) law random matrix theory determine PCs carry signal noise floor. Given nn cells pp features, theoretical noise ceiling λmax=(1+pn)2\lambda_{\max} = \left(1 + \sqrt{\frac{p}{n}}\right)^2 PCs whose variance σi2>λmax\sigma_i^2 > \lambda_{\max} receive non-zero weight; others treated noise zeroed . PC clears threshold (e.g. tiny datasets) function falls back "prop.var" warning. Proportional-variance vs standard-deviation weighting.
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "stdev",                         reduction.name = "umap.wt.sd", verbose = FALSE) p3 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wt.sd", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Weighted UMAP (stdev)") + NoLegend() p2 | p3

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: weight.= "mp" uses random matrix theory identify PCs carry genuine signal noise floor predicted random matrix dimensions. PCs receive non-zero weight. can inspect many PCs retained (non-zero weight):  MP weighting vs proportional-variance weighting.
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "mp",                         reduction.name = "umap.mp", verbose = FALSE) mp_weights <- Misc(pbmc[["umap.mp"]])$weights cat(sprintf("%d / %d PCs above noise floor (non-zero weight)\n",             sum(mp_weights > 0), length(mp_weights))) #> 8 / 50 PCs above noise floor (non-zero weight) barplot(mp_weights, names.arg = seq_along(mp_weights),         xlab = "PC", ylab = "MP weight",         main = "Marchenko-Pastur weights (zero = noise)",         col = ifelse(mp_weights > 0, "#4393C3", "#D1D1D1"),         border = NA, las = 1) p_mp <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.mp", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Weighted UMAP (mp)") + NoLegend() p2 | p_mp

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: weight.factor (0–1) continuously blends unweighted (0) fully weighted (1) embedding: weight.factor controls blend standard fully weighted.
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "prop.var",                         weight.factor = 0.25, reduction.name = "umap.wf25",                         verbose = FALSE) pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "prop.var",                         weight.factor = 0.75, reduction.name = "umap.wf75",                         verbose = FALSE) pa <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.std",  label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("weight.factor = 0") + NoLegend() pb <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wf25", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("weight.factor = 0.25") + NoLegend() pc <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wf75", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("weight.factor = 0.75") + NoLegend() pd <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wt",   label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("weight.factor = 1") + NoLegend() (pa | pb) / (pc | pd)

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: datasets PC 1 explains dramatically variance others, log.scale = TRUE first applies log1p() weights normalising. compresses dynamic range intermediate PCs still contribute layout. Standard (left), fully weighted (centre), log-scaled weights (right).
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "prop.var",                         log.scale = TRUE, reduction.name = "umap.log",                         verbose = FALSE) DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.std", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("Standard") + NoLegend() | DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wt",  label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("prop.var") + NoLegend() | DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.log", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("prop.var + log.scale") + NoLegend()

what: Consistent clustering and UMAP with RunWeightedNeighbors()
title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: common pitfall: clustering one KNN graph visualising another. RunWeightedNeighbors() stores weighted embedding builds KNN SNN graphs one step, clustering UMAP share exactly nearest-neighbour structure. Clustering UMAP derived weighted KNN graph.
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "prop.var",                              prefix = "wt", verbose = FALSE)  pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, graph.name = "wt_snn", resolution = 0.5,                      verbose = FALSE)  pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, graph = "wt_nn", reduction.name = "umap.consistent",                         verbose = FALSE) DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.consistent", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Consistent weighted clustering + UMAP") + NoLegend()

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: RunLocalPCAUMAP() principled alternative global variance weighting. Rather rescaling PC axes globally, measures distance every pair neighbours locally-fitted PCA basis — capturing predominant direction variation cell’s neighbourhood (e.g. tangent trajectory) de-emphasising transverse noise. Algorithm: Find global kk nearest neighbours every cell PCA space (RANN). cell ii, centre kk-neighbourhood compute compact SVD. Re-express displacement vectors neighbours local local.dims principal directions. Report Euclidean norm local basis refined distance. Pass n×kn \times k index/distance matrix directly uwot::umap precomputed k-NN graph. Standard UMAP (left) vs local PCA UMAP (right). local PCA approach can reveal trajectory-like structure compressed standard UMAP distance metric insensitive predominant local direction variation.
code: set.seed(42) pbmc <- RunLocalPCAUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 30,                         reduction.name = "lp.umap", verbose = FALSE) p_std <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.std",  label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Standard UMAP") + NoLegend() p_lp  <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "lp.umap",   label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Local PCA UMAP") + NoLegend() p_std | p_lp

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: quantitative evaluation weighting schemes (including "mp" RunLocalPCAUMAP) independent protein-based ground truth (cbmc CITE-seq), see Benchmark article.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Lachlan Dryburgh. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dryburgh L (2026). wUMAP: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP Seurat Objects. R package version 0.1.0.
code: @Manual{,   title = {wUMAP: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects},   author = {Lachlan Dryburgh},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0}, }

what: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: wUMAP provides RunWeightedUMAP(), drop-replacement Seurat’s RunUMAP() scales principal component weight derived variance explained running manifold learning. gives biologically informative PCs influence final embedding.

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: Standard UMAP treats every PC dimension equally computing cell–cell distances. practice, PC1 might explain 15% variance PC30 explains 0.3% — yet contribute equally distance matrix. Weighting PCs variance explained amplifies axes real biological variation suppresses noise-dominated dimensions.

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: Dependencies (uwot, SeuratObject) installed automatically.
code: # Install from GitHub (requires remotes) remotes::install_github("LachlanD/weightedUMAP")

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
code: library(Seurat) library(wUMAP)  # Assumes pbmc has PCA already computed (e.g. via RunPCA()) pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var") DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "wt.umap", label = TRUE)

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: weight.factor (0–1) blends standard UMAP (0) fully weighted UMAP (1):
code: # Standard UMAP — all PCs equal pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         weight.factor = 0)  # Half-strength weighting pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         weight.factor = 0.5)  # Full weighting (default) pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         weight.factor = 1)

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var")    # default pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev") pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "none")

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: log.scale = TRUE, log1p() applied weights re-normalised embedding. compresses gap high- low-variance PCs intermediate PCs receive relatively influence, still preserving rank ordering weights.
code: # Full weighting, no log scaling (default) pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         log.scale = FALSE)  # Log-scaled weights — intermediate PCs get relatively more influence pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         log.scale = TRUE)

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: key difference PC scores scaled distances computed: Standard UMAP (weight.= "none") — every PC contributes equally, noisy high-index PCs can blur cluster boundaries. Weighted UMAP (weight.= "prop.var") — PCs multiplied weight UMAP runs, dominant axes biological variation drive layout.
code: library(patchwork)  # Run both on the same object pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "none",                         reduction.name = "umap.std") pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         reduction.name = "wt.umap")  # Compare side by side p1 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.std", label = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Standard UMAP") p2 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "wt.umap",  label = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Weighted UMAP (prop.var)") p1 | p2

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: returned object gains new DimReduc reduction.name. misc slot stores chosen weight., weights vector, source.reduction, dims.used reproducibility.
code: RunWeightedUMAP(   object,   reduction      = "pca",       # source reduction   dims           = NULL,        # e.g. 1:30; NULL uses all available dims   weight.by      = "prop.var",  # weighting scheme (see table above)   weight.factor  = 1,           # 0 = standard UMAP, 1 = fully weighted   log.scale      = FALSE,       # compress weight dynamic range with log1p()   graph          = NULL,        # name of KNN graph from RunWeightedNeighbors()                                 #   e.g. "wt_nn"; overrides dims/weight.by/reduction   reduction.name = "wt.umap",   # name stored in the Seurat object   reduction.key  = "wtUMAP_",   # column prefix: wtUMAP_1, wtUMAP_2, ...   n.neighbors    = 30L,   n.components   = 2L,   metric         = "euclidean",   min.dist       = 0.3,   spread         = 1,   seed.use       = 42L,   verbose        = TRUE,   ...                           # passed to uwot::umap() )

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: default, FindNeighbors() RunUMAP() build neighbour graph — unweighted PCA scores — topology used clustering one used UMAP embedding can differ. RunWeightedNeighbors() computes weighted PC embeddings , stores "<prefix>.pca" reduction, builds KNN/SNN graphs weighted space. call RunWeightedUMAP(graph = "wt_nn", ...), looks stored weighted embedding runs UMAP directly — clustering visualisation operate weighted PC space k. Note: graph argument tells RunWeightedUMAP weighted space use (convention "wt_nn" → "wt.pca"). actual UMAP computed embedding, binary adjacency values graph. Compare standard (inconsistent) workflow:
code: library(Seurat) library(wUMAP) library(patchwork)  # Step 1 — build weighted KNN/SNN graphs (k = 20 by default) #           stores 'wt.pca' embedding and 'wt_nn' / 'wt_snn' graphs pbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                               k.param = 20, prefix = "wt")  # Step 2 — cluster on the weighted SNN graph pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, graph.name = "wt_snn")  # Step 3 — UMAP in the same weighted space #           set n.neighbors to match the k.param used above pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, graph = "wt_nn", n.neighbors = 20,                          reduction.name = "wt.umap")  DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "wt.umap", label = TRUE) # Standard workflow — neighbour graphs computed independently pbmc <- FindNeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30)           # unweighted, k = 20 pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc) pbmc <- RunUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30)                 # separate NN computation

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: MIT

what: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
text: Computes UMAP using local PCA metric: cell, distance \(k\) nearest neighbours re-measured locally-fitted PCA basis built neighbourhood.  captures anisotropic local structure (e.g. tangent direction trajectory) de-emphasises transverse noise, producing richer distance measure single global Euclidean metric.

title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
code: RunLocalPCAUMAP(   object,   reduction = "pca",   dims = NULL,   k.param = 30L,   local.dims = NULL,   reduction.name = "lp.umap",   reduction.key = "lpUMAP_",   n.components = 2L,   min.dist = 0.3,   spread = 1,   seed.use = 42L,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
text: object Seurat object PCA already run. reduction Name PCA reduction use. Default: "pca". dims Integer vector PC dimensions use. Default: available. k.param Number nearest neighbours local PCA. Default: 30L. local.dims Number local PCA directions retain computing refined distances.  Must \(\leq\) k.param. Default: NULL (uses k.param directions). reduction.name Name store UMAP result. Default: "lp.umap". reduction.key Column-name prefix UMAP dimensions. Default: "lpUMAP_". n.components Number UMAP dimensions. Default: 2L. min.dist UMAP min_dist parameter. Default: 0.3. spread UMAP spread parameter. Default: 1. seed.use Random seed. Default: 42L. verbose Print progress messages. Default: TRUE. ... Additional arguments forwarded umap.

title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
text: input Seurat object new DimReduc stored reduction.name.

title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
text: Find global k.param nearest neighbours every cell supplied PCA embedding (using RANN). cell \(\), collect \(k\)-neighbourhood, centre neighbourhood mean, compute compact SVD. Re-express displacement vectors \(\) neighbour local local.dims principal directions. Report Euclidean norm local-PC space refined distance. Pass \(n \times k\) index/distance matrices directly umap precomputed k-NN graph.

title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(Seurat) library(wUMAP)  # Assumes pbmc has PCA already run pbmc <- RunLocalPCAUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 30,                         reduction.name = "lp.umap")  # Compare to standard UMAP p1 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap",    label = TRUE) + ggtitle("Standard") p2 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "lp.umap", label = TRUE) + ggtitle("Local PCA") p1 | p2 } # }

what: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
title: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
text: Scales principal component variance-derived weight (weighting RunWeightedUMAP), stores result new DimReduc, builds KNN/SNN graph via Seurat's FindNeighbors().  resulting graphs can passed directly FindClusters , via graph argument RunWeightedUMAP, UMAP — ensuring clustering visualisation share exactly nearest-neighbour structure.

title: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
code: RunWeightedNeighbors(   object,   reduction = "pca",   dims = NULL,   weight.by = c("prop.var", "stdev", "mp", "none"),   k.param = 20L,   reduction.name = "wt.pca",   reduction.key = "wtPCA_",   prefix = "wt",   weight.factor = 1,   log.scale = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
text: object Seurat object PCA (linear) reduction already computed. reduction Name dimensional reduction use input. Default: "pca". dims Integer vector dimensions use. NULL (default), available dimensions used warning raised 50. weight.Weighting scheme applied PC scores UMAP. One : "prop.var" Proportion variance explained (sdev^2 / sum(sdev^2)). Default. "stdev" Standard deviation, normalised (sdev / sum(sdev)). "none" weighting; equivalent standard UMAP PCA scores. k.param Number nearest neighbours KNN graph. Default: 20L. reduction.name Name store intermediate weighted-PC embedding object.  Default: "wt.pca". reduction.key Column-name prefix weighted-PC dimensions. Default: "wtPCA_". prefix Prefix two graphs stored Seurat object: <prefix>_nn (KNN, used UMAP) <prefix>_snn (SNN, used clustering). Pass prefix FindClusters(graph.name = "<prefix>_snn") RunWeightedUMAP(graph = "<prefix>_nn"). Default: "wt". weight.factor number [0, 1] controlling influence weighting scheme.  1 (default) full variance-derived weights applied.  0 PCs contribute equally (standard nearest-neighbour graph).  Must match weight.factor used subsequent RunWeightedUMAP call keep clustering visualisation space. log.scale Logical. TRUE, log1p() applied weights weight.factor interpolation.  match value used subsequent RunWeightedUMAP call. Default: FALSE. verbose Print progress messages per-PC weights? Default: TRUE. ... Additional arguments forwarded FindNeighbors.

title: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
text: input Seurat object : new DimReduc stored reduction.name containing variance-weighted PC embeddings. Two neighbour graphs (<prefix>_nn <prefix>_snn) suitable downstream FindClusters() RunWeightedUMAP(graph = ...).

title: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(Seurat) library(wUMAP)  # 1. Compute the weighted KNN/SNN graphs (same space for clustering + UMAP) pbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                              prefix = "wt")  # 2. Cluster on the weighted SNN graph pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, graph.name = "wt_snn")  # 3. UMAP from the same weighted KNN graph — topology is identical to step 2 pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, graph = "wt_nn", n.neighbors = 20,                         reduction.name = "wt.umap")  DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "wt.umap", label = TRUE)  # Use log.scale to compress the weight dynamic range so intermediate PCs # contribute more alongside the dominant early PCs pbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                              log.scale = TRUE, prefix = "wt.log") pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, graph.name = "wt.log_snn") pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, graph = "wt.log_nn",                         reduction.name = "wt.umap.log") } # }

what: Run Variance-Weighted UMAP on a Seurat Object — RunWeightedUMAP
title: Run Variance-Weighted UMAP on a Seurat Object — RunWeightedUMAP
text: Scales principal component embedding weight derived variance explained component, runs UMAP via uwot. result stored new DimReduc returned Seurat object.

title: Run Variance-Weighted UMAP on a Seurat Object — RunWeightedUMAP
code: RunWeightedUMAP(   object,   reduction = "pca",   dims = NULL,   weight.by = c("prop.var", "stdev", "mp", "none"),   graph = NULL,   reduction.name = "wt.umap",   reduction.key = "wtUMAP_",   n.neighbors = 30L,   n.components = 2L,   metric = "euclidean",   min.dist = 0.3,   spread = 1,   seed.use = 42L,   weight.factor = 1,   log.scale = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Run Variance-Weighted UMAP on a Seurat Object — RunWeightedUMAP
text: object Seurat object PCA (linear) reduction already computed. reduction Name dimensional reduction use input. Default: "pca". dims Integer vector dimensions use. NULL (default), available dimensions used warning raised 50. weight.Weighting scheme applied PC scores UMAP. One : "prop.var" Proportion variance explained (sdev^2 / sum(sdev^2)). Default. "stdev" Standard deviation, normalised (sdev / sum(sdev)). "none" weighting; equivalent standard UMAP PCA scores. graph Optional name precomputed Seurat KNN graph produced RunWeightedNeighbors() (e.g. "wt_nn").  supplied, UMAP run weighted PC embedding generated graph (stored RunWeightedNeighbors() "<prefix>.pca", <prefix> derived stripping _nn suffix graph name).  ensures UMAP topology computed exactly weighted space clustering graphs.  dims, weight., reduction, metric arguments ignored graph supplied.  Set n.neighbors match k.param used RunWeightedNeighbors().  Default: NULL. reduction.name Name store new UMAP reduction object. Default: "wt.umap". reduction.key Column‐name prefix UMAP dimensions, e.g. "wtUMAP_" produces column names wtUMAP_1, wtUMAP_2, … n.neighbors Number nearest neighbours used UMAP. Default: 30. n.components Number UMAP dimensions compute. Default: 2. metric Distance metric passed uwot::umap(). Default: "euclidean". min.dist Effective minimum distance embedded points. Default: 0.3. spread Effective scale embedded points. Default: 1. seed.use Random seed reproducibility. Set NULL skip set.seed(). Default: 42. weight.factor number [0, 1] scales influence chosen weighting scheme.  1 (default) full variance-derived weights applied.  0 PCs contribute equally, equivalent weight.= "none" (standard UMAP).  Intermediate values blend uniformly-weighted fully-weighted embeddings. log.scale Logical. TRUE, log1p() applied weights weight.factor interpolation, compressing dynamic range intermediate PCs receive relatively influence.  Ignored weight.= "none".  Default: FALSE. verbose Print progress messages per-PC weights? Default: TRUE. ... Additional arguments forwarded uwot::umap().

title: Run Variance-Weighted UMAP on a Seurat Object — RunWeightedUMAP
text: input Seurat object new DimReduc named reduction.name appended. misc slot reduction stores: weight.— chosen weighting scheme weights    — numeric vector weights (one per dimension used) source.reduction — name source reduction dims.used — integer vector dimensions used

title: Run Variance-Weighted UMAP on a Seurat Object — RunWeightedUMAP
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(Seurat) library(wUMAP) library(patchwork)  # Assumes pbmc has PCA already run  # Standard UMAP — all PCs weighted equally pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "none",                         reduction.name = "umap.std")  # Weighted UMAP — PCs scaled by proportion of variance explained pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         reduction.name = "wt.umap")  # Log-scaled weights — compresses dynamic range so intermediate PCs # contribute more relative to the dominant PCs pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         log.scale = TRUE, reduction.name = "wt.umap.log")  # Standard deviation weighting pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev",                         reduction.name = "wt.umap.sd")  # Compare standard vs weighted side by side p1 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.std",   label = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Standard UMAP") p2 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "wt.umap",    label = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Weighted UMAP (prop.var)") p3 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "wt.umap.log", label = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Weighted UMAP (prop.var, log.scale)") p1 | p2 } # }
